DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL A PSYCHOLOGICAL EXAMINATION -- SHANNON BETTIS NAKABAYASHI APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY. NO APPEARANCE FOR PLAINTIFF. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. NOTWITHSTANDING THE PROPOSED STIPULATION BY COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF THAT NO EXPERT TESTIMONY WILL BE OFFERED AT TRIAL AND PLAINTIFF WILL NOT CLAIM EMOTIONAL INJURIES OVER AND ABOVE THOSE USUALLY ASSOCIATED WITH THE ALLEGED INJURIES SET FORTH IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT HAS DEMONSTRATED EXCEPTIONAL CIRCUMSTANCES JUSTIFYING A LIMITED EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF AT THE OFFICES OF DR. JAMES D. MISSETT, 1187 UNIVERSITY DRIVE, SUITE 8, MENLO PARK, CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO ARRANGE FOR ROUNDTRIP TRANSPORTATION FROM PLAINTIFF'S HOME TO THE OFFICE OF DR. MISSETT. THE DURATION OF THE EXAMINATION SHALL NOT EXCEED FIVE HOURS. THE EXAMINATION SHALL TAKE PLACE ON DECEMBER 15, 2004 UNLESS PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO ATTEND DUE TO SCHEDULED CHEMOTHERAPY TREATMENTS OR THE EFFECTS OF SUCH TREATMENTS. THE EXAMINATION SHALL BE COMPLETED ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 27, 2004. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
